findings.title: Force-unwrap on new sponsorURL Link — pre-existing pattern but newly introduced instance
findings.description: The PR introduces `Link("Sponsors", destination: URL(string: DirectConfig.sponsorURL)!)` at line 69. This force-unwraps `URL(string:)`, which returns an optional. The development rules file prohibits this unconditionally.
findings.rule_citation: docs/development-rules.md, 'The Ten Commandments' #5: 'Type Safety: No force unwrapping, handle all optionals properly'; also 'Never Do This': '❌ Force unwrap optionals (!)'; also 'Optionals & Safety' section: '// ❌ NEVER force unwrap — let name = food!.name  // Don\'t do this!'
findings.pre_existing_context: The surrounding lines in AboutView.swift at the base commit (519e0589) already contain identical force-unwrap patterns on facebookURL (removed), donateURL, crowdinURL (removed), githubURL, faqURL, and the mailto URL. These are all pre-existing. The sponsorURL force-unwrap is newly added by this PR and follows the pre-existing pattern rather than cleaning it up.
findings.disposition: The violation is real and newly introduced by this diff. However, because 5 other identical force-unwraps were already present in the same file at the base commit and the PR does not otherwise touch safety hygiene, this is classified as a low-severity carry-forward rather than a regression.

residual_risks.description: The five remaining force-unwraps on URL(string:) literals in AboutView.swift (lines 36, 45, 53, 61, 69 in the current file) are technically violations of the no-force-unwrap rule in docs/development-rules.md. They are all pre-existing and untouched by this PR, so they are not flagged as primary findings, but they represent lingering technical debt against a documented project rule.
residual_risks.rule_citation: docs/development-rules.md, 'Never Do This': '❌ Force unwrap optionals (!)'
